verses.text: Account us 'ministers of Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God'; it is required that a steward be 'found faithful.' Faithful trust. The chief mark of a steward is faithfulness.
Account us 'ministers of Christ, and stewards of the mysteries of God'; it is required that a steward be 'found faithful.' Faithful trust. The chief mark of a steward is faithfulness.
I judge not myself; the Lord shall judge, bringing 'to light the hidden things of darkness.' His judgment. We leave the verdict to God, who sees all.
I judge not myself; the Lord shall judge, bringing 'to light the hidden things of darkness.' His judgment. We leave the verdict to God, who sees all.
I judge not myself; the Lord shall judge, bringing 'to light the hidden things of darkness.' His judgment. We leave the verdict to God, who sees all.
Who makes thee to differ? 'what hast thou that thou didst not receive?' No boasting. All we have is a gift, leaving no room for pride.
Who makes thee to differ? 'what hast thou that thou didst not receive?' No boasting. All we have is a gift, leaving no room for pride.
Ye reign as kings; 'we are made a spectacle... we are fools for Christ's sake.' Suffering apostles. The servants of Christ bear shame the proud avoid.
Ye reign as kings; 'we are made a spectacle... we are fools for Christ's sake.' Suffering apostles. The servants of Christ bear shame the proud avoid.
Ye reign as kings; 'we are made a spectacle... we are fools for Christ's sake.' Suffering apostles. The servants of Christ bear shame the proud avoid.
We hunger, are buffeted, reviled, yet bless; 'made as the filth of the world.' Patient suffering. They return good for evil amid scorn.
We hunger, are buffeted, reviled, yet bless; 'made as the filth of the world.' Patient suffering. They return good for evil amid scorn.
We hunger, are buffeted, reviled, yet bless; 'made as the filth of the world.' Patient suffering. They return good for evil amid scorn.
I warn you 'as my beloved sons'; in Christ I begat you; 'be ye followers of me.' A father's plea. He corrects them in love, not to shame.
I warn you 'as my beloved sons'; in Christ I begat you; 'be ye followers of me.' A father's plea. He corrects them in love, not to shame.
I warn you 'as my beloved sons'; in Christ I begat you; 'be ye followers of me.' A father's plea. He corrects them in love, not to shame.
I send Timothy to remind you of my ways; I will come, 'if the Lord will.' Coming visit. He longs to set them right in person.
I send Timothy to remind you of my ways; I will come, 'if the Lord will.' Coming visit. He longs to set them right in person.
I send Timothy to remind you of my ways; I will come, 'if the Lord will.' Coming visit. He longs to set them right in person.
'The kingdom of God is not in word, but in power'; shall I come with a rod, or in love? Real power. True religion shows in power, not talk.
